title: Is it normal that an AI tool is giving me better chart breakdowns than most YouTubers?
author: No_Jicama5917
selftext:
I’ve been testing an AI assistant that analyzes my TradingView charts and gives structured breakdowns (market structure, liquidity zones, invalidation, etc.).  
I expected it to be trash, but some of the explanations were surprisingly solid.

Now I’m questioning whether I’ve been overcomplicating my analysis for years.

Has anyone else noticed this with AI tools?

https://reddit.com/link/1rlsfjf/video/67qqys7ibang1/player

comments.author: Kindly_Preference_54
comments.body: Of course it is normal - it is actually expected.

comments.author: QuirkyChipmunk1414
comments.body:
Yeah, that’s actually becoming pretty normal.

AI is really good at structuring information and removing a lot of the bias that humans add. Many YouTubers mix analysis with opinion, while AI can just focus on structure, liquidity, and probabilities.

Tools like AlphaMind AI are built exactly for that. It analyzes forex and commodities charts and helps break down market structure, potential setups, and risk context without the hype.

It won’t replace your judgment, but it can make the analysis a lot cleaner and faster.

replies.author: No_Jicama5917
replies.body: There's a clear logo on the video... I'm using Chartmentor.

replies.author: ukSurreyGuy
replies.body:
you designed [ChartMentor ](https://www.reddit.com/r/technicalanalysis/s/wvaRaosugB)

comments.author: Automatic-Essay2175
comments.body: Not using your product. Also not taking advice from YouTubers.
